puzzle.title: The Fading Gardens of Prosperity
puzzle.intro: In a land blessed with abundant bounty, a community flourished, living amidst signs of divine grace. Their prosperity was a testament to their Lord's favor, a gift meant to inspire gratitude and obedience. Yet, despite the clear indications of their blessings, a turning point arrived, challenging their appreciation for the source of their immense wealth and comfort.
clues: We were blessed with two gardens, one on the right and one on the left, abundant with produce.
We were commanded to eat from the provision of our Lord and be grateful to Him for a good land and a forgiving Lord.
We grew arrogant and turned away from the path of gratitude, boasting of our wealth and power.
We demanded that our Lord increase the distance between our journeys, showing disdain for the ease He had provided.
We were warned by a messenger, but we rejected the message and continued in our heedlessness.
Our great dam, which sustained our prosperity, was ultimately broken, leading to a devastating flood.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: A destructive flood from a broken dam
trial.answer: A destructive flood from a broken dam

outcome.label: Outcome
options: We were transformed into apes and pigs
We were saved and migrated to a new land
We were scattered and our land became barren
We built a new, more prosperous kingdom
outcome.answer: We were scattered and our land became barren

puzzle.arabic: فَأَعْرَضُوا۟ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَيْلَ ٱلْعَرِمِ وَبَدَّلْنَـٰهُم بِجَنَّتَيْهِمْ جَنَّتَيْنِ ذَوَاتَىْ أُكُلٍ خَمْطٍۢ وَأَثْلٍۢ وَشَىْءٍۢ مِّن سِدْرٍۢ قَلِيلٍۢ
puzzle.verse: But they turned away [refusing], so We sent upon them the flood of the dam, and We replaced their two [fields of] gardens with gardens of bitter fruit, tamarisks and something of sparse lote trees. (34:16)
